signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Crude Oil + Positioning
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7169 testing downside despite RBA raising rates to 4.35% and WTI crude surging +4.39% to $105.2. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) represents a crowded institutional trade failing to respond to hawkish catalysts. Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35% provides a -60bp carry advantage for AUD, yet price action softens below 0.7150 intraday. Implication: Bullish positioning is overextended; failure to rally on a 'precautionary hike' and oil spike suggests a distribution phase and long-unwind risk.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) shows speculators heavily fading the RBNZ despite a hawkish market bias for the next meeting on 27 May. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% creates a +150bp spread favoring USD, but the extreme spec short positioning (lowest long% in G8) creates significant short-squeeze risk if RBNZ signals further tightening. NZD/USD at 0.5873 remains vulnerable to a sharp reversal if OIS pricing firms.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Swiss CPI + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7840 following Swiss CPI acceleration to 0.6% YoY. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% maintains a massive +375bp carry advantage for USD, but retail sentiment is 83% long, diverging from COT LF net short -6,117. Institutional shorts are positioned for CHF safe-haven recovery while retail is heavily chasing the carry trade. Implication: High risk of retail capitulation if CPI-driven CHF strength breaks the 0.7800 handle.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.731 vs 252d norm of -0.138 (z=+1.86σ). This significant decoupling indicates EUR is trading as a risk-proxy rather than its historical inverse relationship with EU equities. EUR COT LF net short -18,293 (45.4% long) remains neutral-to-short while Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) supports USD. Implication: EUR/USD upside is structurally capped by the carry deficit despite the equity correlation shift. Watch 1.1650 for support.
